message:
fix(ext-session): correct changeset/patchset binary format, add change coalescing, and fix conflict apply semantics

The session extension's binary wire format now matches the real SQLite
session extension byte-for-byte.  Key corrections:

Binary format fixes:
- Distinguish changeset ('T' = 0x54) vs patchset ('P' = 0x50) table
  header bytes.  Previously both formats used the same 'T' header,
  which meant patchset blobs could not be decoded by the real SQLite
  sqlite3changeset_apply_v2 entry point.
- Emit the indirect flag byte (0x00) after every operation byte in both
  changeset and patchset row encoding.  The prior encoding omitted this
  byte, causing a one-byte offset error in every row payload.
- Patchset DELETE rows now emit only primary-key column values (skipping
  non-PK columns), matching the SQLite patchset spec.  The decoder was
  updated symmetrically to reconstruct the sparse old_values vector.

Change coalescing (net-effect reduction):
- build_changeset_impl() now coalesces multiple tracked changes for the
  same primary key into a single net-effect row using the new
  PendingRowChange state machine.  INSERT+UPDATE collapses to INSERT
  with the final values; INSERT+DELETE cancels out entirely;
  UPDATE+UPDATE merges; UPDATE+DELETE collapses to DELETE; etc.
- Helper functions: materialize_sparse_update(), canonical_old_values(),
  canonical_new_values(), primary_key_identity() support the merge.

Conflict apply fix:
- SimpleTarget::apply_update now compares old_values element-wise,
  treating ChangesetValue::Undefined as "don't care" rather than
  requiring exact row equality.  This matches SQLite's conflict
  detection behavior where only defined columns are checked.

New unit tests:
- test_session_changeset_coalesces_net_row_effects validates the
  INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE coalescing pipeline.
- Updated assertions for patchset header bytes, patchset DELETE
  encoding, and apply outcome counts throughout the existing test suite.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
